problem text: The surfaces of a $3 \times 3 \times 3$ cube (initially white) are colored red and then divided into $27$ $1 \times 1 \times 1$ miniature cubes. One small cube is chosen at random and is rolled. The face that lands face-up is red. What is the probability that the chosen cube was a corner cube?
problem solution: We restrict our sample space to cubes with at least one red face. There are $54$ total red faces on the cube, as each face of the large cube has an area of $9$. There are $8$ corner cubes, each having $3$ red faces. These account for $24$ of the $54$ total red faces. Thus, the probability is $\dfrac{24}{54} = \dfrac{4}{9}$.
problem hint: We restrict our sample space to cubes with at least one red face. How many red faces are there in total?
